name: integrate-ai

metadata.name: integrate-ai

documentation:
# integrate.ai Command Line Interface

A CLI that enables users to interact with integrate.ai's software.

This tool allows users to manage client and sdk packages. Use the integate.ai sdk to seamlessly integrate federated learning and analytics workflows directly into your product.

## Installation

integrate-ai requires `Python >= 3.8`

```
pip install integrate-ai
```

## Quick Start

Create a new IAI token through the integrate.ai UI.

### Install integrate-ai-sdk

```
iai sdk install --token <IAI_TOKEN> 
```

### Pull integrate.ai docker client

```
iai client pull --token <IAI_TOKEN> 
```

### Run integrate.ai training session

```
iai client train --token <IAI_TOKEN> --session <SESSION_ID> --train_path <PATH_TO_TRAINING_DATA> --test_path <PATH_TO_TEST_DATA> --batch_size 5
```

modules.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client
documentation:
Module containing docker interface functionality.

This contains a `DockerClient` class that will be used to interface with the docker client on 
  the user's system.
Having a docker client installed on the system is a prerequisite for this to work.
Copyright (C) Integrate.ai, Inc. All rights reserved.

classes.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient.__init__
documentation:
Initialize.

Args:
    token (str): IAI token.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient.login
documentation:
Retrieves authentication to connect to ECR repo and performs docker login.

Returns:
    login_response (Dict): Contains response information returned from docker login.

Raises:
    DockerException: If error performing docker login command.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient.get_repo_name
documentation:
Retrieves the client image name to pull.

Returns:
    name (str): name of the docker image to retrieve.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient._auth_dict
documentation:
Returns authorization dictionary for docker commands using ECR repo.

Returns:
    auth_dict (Dict): contains authorization dictionary for ECR connection

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient.filter_image_by_tag_part
documentation:
Determines if `tag_part` is found in image tags.

Args:
    image (Image): image to check tags for.
    tag_part (str): Checked against image tags to see if is contained in first part of tag.

Returns:
    tag_part_contained (bool): True if `tag_part` found in tags, False otherwise.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient.get_local_versions
documentation:
Returns the local images that contain `tag_part` as part of one of their tags.

Args:
    tag_part (str): prefix to filter images by.

Returns:
    image_list (List[Image]): list of images on user's system containing `tag_part`.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient.get_latest_version_of_image
documentation:
Returns the latest version number from a list of comparable images.

Note: If 'latest' is the only version found (no numbered version) or list is empty, will return `None`

Args:
    images (List[Image]): list of images to search for latest version.

Returns:
    version_no (str) latest version number found if available. Otherwise `None`.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient.get_versions
documentation:
Returns a list of available versions from the ecr repository.

Returns:
    versions (List[string]): list of available docker images to pull.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient.delete_images
documentation:
Deletes images contained in a list

Args:
    images (List[Image]): list of images to delete.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient.pull
documentation:
Pulls docker image corresponding to specified repo and tag.

Args:
    repo (str): image name to pull.
    tag (str): tag of image to pull.
    platform (str): Platform of image default to linux/amd64
Returns:
    pull_response (Dict[str,str]): Response Dict returned from docker pull request.

Raises:
    DockerException: If error in docker pull command.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient.get_latest_available_version
documentation:
Returns the latest available version of the client docker available in repo.

Returns:
    latest_version (str): latest client image version number available in repo.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient.run
documentation:
Starts a container with given image name and configurations.

Args:
    image str: image name to run the container
    detach bool: whether or not the process should return immediately
    options Dict[str, str]: a dict of options accepted by `docker run` command.

Returns:
    run_response (Dict[str,str]): Response Dict returned from docker run command.

Raises:
    DockerException: If error in docker run command.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.docker_client.DockerClient.get_container
documentation:
Gets container with given name.

Args:
    container_name str: name of container we are searching for

Returns:
    Container: class representation of container object

Raises:
    DockerException: If error in getting container.

Class to interact with docker client.

This class performs various docker operations such as login, pull, rmi, etc.

modules.name: integrate_ai.client
documentation: Copyright (C) Integrate.ai, Inc. All rights reserved.

functions.name: integrate_ai.client.pull
documentation:
Pull the federated learning docker client.
Defaults to the latest CPU version. Docker must be running for this command to work.

functions.name: integrate_ai.client.version
documentation: The currently installed version of the docker client image.

functions.name: integrate_ai.client.list
documentation: List all available docker client images versions to pull.

functions.name: integrate_ai.client.log
documentation: This command fetches logs of the client docker container for debugging purposes.

functions.name: integrate_ai.client.train
documentation:
Join a training session using the docker client.
The client docker container will be deleted on completion.
The following environment variables will be passed into the docker container:
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_SESSION_TOKEN, AWS_REGION.
This way code running inside the container can take advantage of AWS APIs, e.g. reading
data from S3.

functions.name: integrate_ai.client.eda
documentation:
Join a eda session using the docker client.
The client docker container will be deleted on completion.
The following environment variables will be passed into the docker container:
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_SESSION_TOKEN, AWS_REGION.
This way code running inside the container can take advantage of AWS APIs, e.g. reading
data from S3.

functions.name: integrate_ai.client._get_image_name
documentation:
Get image name used to spin up fl-client.
If `IAI_ALT_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME` is set, use the given image name, otherwise read the latest version exist in fl-client repo.
Args:
    docker_client (DockerClient): A DockerClient object
Returns:
    image_name (str): image name for fl-client

functions.name: integrate_ai.client.mount_and_start_docker
documentation:
Mount data paths to docker and start docker container

Args:
    docker_client (DockerClient): A DockerClient object
    container_name (str): Name of the container
    session (str): Session id
    command (str):  Can be either eda or train
    kwargs:
        train_path (Dict): Training data path for train command
        test_path (Dict): Testing data path for train command
        dataset_path (Dict): Data path for eda command

Returns:
    mounted_data_path (Dict): Mounted data path
    local_log_path (str): Full path to the client log file

functions.name: integrate_ai.client.mount_data_for_train
documentation:
Mount data paths for train command

Args:
    mount_path (DockerClient): A DockerClient object
    volumes (str): Volumes for docker container
    kwargs:
        train_path (Dict): Training data path for train command
        test_path (Dict): Testing data path for train command

Returns:
    volumes (Dict): Binded volumes for local paths
    mounted_data_path (Dict): Mounted data paths

functions.name: integrate_ai.client.mount_data_for_eda
documentation:
Mount data paths for eda command

Args:
    mount_path (DockerClient): A DockerClient object
    volumes (str): Volumes for docker container
    kwargs:
        dataset_path (Dict): Dataset_path data path for eda command

Returns:
    volumes (Dict): Binded volumes for local paths
    mounted_data_path (Dict): Mounted data paths

functions.name: integrate_ai.client.set_env_var
documentation:
Update the dict of existing environment variables to include `env_var_name`,
if it is set in the current environment.

Args:
    environment (Dict): the dictionary of existing environment variables
    env_var_name (str): the new env variable to add

functions.name: integrate_ai.client.excute_command_and_close_docker
documentation:
Excute eda or train command and close the container when complete

Args:
    container : A Container object
    cmd : Command to be execute
    environment : AWS environment variable to pass in the container
    local_log_path :  Full path to the client log file
    remove_after_complete: Remove the container after complete when equals True
    verbose: Log verbose for container

documentation: Sub command for managing client related operations.

modules.name: integrate_ai.utils.error_handling

functions.name: integrate_ai.utils.error_handling.check_for_IntegrateAIException
documentation:
Raises an IntegrateAIException from a response.

If response has an error status code, raises a custom IntegrateAIException instead
  of the original Exception.

Args:
    response (Response): HTTP response to check for error status and raise custom
      message for.

Raises:
    IntegrateAIException: Customized IntegrateAI exception for the HTTP Exception.

classes.name: integrate_ai.utils.error_handling.IntegrateAIException
documentation: The general exception for request and response errors.

documentation: The general exception for docker-related errors.

functions.name: integrate_ai.utils.typer_utils.toggle_prompt_wrapper
documentation:
Decorates a function to remove prompt as a kwarg if disable_prompts is 'true'.

Args:
    option_func (Callable): function to wrap.
    disable_promtps (str): whether to disable prompts or not ('true' if prompts should be disabled).

Returns:
    decorated function (Callable): either same function as option_func or same function with prompt kwarg removed.

modules.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client
documentation:
Module containing REST interface functionality.

This contains a `RestClient` class that will be used to interface with the IAI REST API.
Copyright (C) Integrate.ai, Inc. All rights reserved.

classes.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient.__init__
documentation:
Initiates a client given a valid decoded IAI token object.

Args:
    token (str): IAI token.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient._headers
documentation: Add authenticating headers for use with REST API.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient._decode_jwt_token
documentation: Decodes a JWT token.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient._get_api_endpoint
documentation: Returns API endpoint.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient._get_gateway_endpoint
documentation: Returns Gateway endpoint.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient.make_get_request
documentation:
Performs a GET request using client.

Args:
    url (str): URL to make GET request to.
    body (Dict): body to include in GET request.
    headers (Dict): The headers to be added to the GET request.
        Defaults to the integrate.ai API Authorization headers.

Returns:
    response (requests.Response): response object from GET request.

Raises:
    IntegrateAIException: If error in GET request.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient.get_ecr_token
documentation:
Retrieves an ECR token using IAI token.

Returns:
    ecr_response (Dict[str,str]): response to ECR GET request.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient.get_ecr_repo
documentation: Retrieves the ecr repo name for a package

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient.get_ecr_versions
documentation: Retrieves the versions of an image from the ecr repo

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient.get_latest_ecr_version
documentation: Retrieves the latest version of an image in ecr

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient.get_pip_install_command
documentation:
Get pip install command to install sdk.
Args:
    version (str): Version of sdk to install.
    package (str): Package name to install.
Returns:
        A Dict of the form
        ```
        {
            "pipInstallCommand":"<pip_install_command>",
        }
        ```
Raises:
    IntegrateAIException: Customized IntegrateAI exception for the HTTP Exception.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient.get_package_versions
documentation:
Get package versions.
Args:
    package (str): Name of package that has a list of available versions.
Returns:
        A Dict of the form
        ```
    {
        "package_name": "<package-name>",
        "package_versions": [
            {
                "version": <version_number_1>,
                "revision": <revision_string>,
                "status": "Published"
            },
            {
                "version": <version_number_2>,
                "revision": <revision_string>,
                "status": "Published"
            }
        ]
    }
        ```
Raises:
    IntegrateAIException: Customized IntegrateAI exception for the HTTP Exception.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient.register_on_prem_taskrunner
documentation:
Get package versions.
Args:
    package (str): Name of package that has a list of available versions.
Returns:
        A Dict of the form
        ```
    {
        "activation_id": <activation_id>,
        "activation_code": <activation_code>,
        "region": <region>,
        "cluster": <cluster>
    }
        ```
Raises:
    IntegrateAIException: Customized IntegrateAI exception for the HTTP Exception.

methods.name: integrate_ai.utils.rest_client.RestClient.deregister_on_prem_taskrunner
documentation:
Get package versions.
Args:
    package (str): Name of package that has a list of available versions.
Returns:
        A Dict of the form
        ```
    {
    }
        ```
Raises:
    IntegrateAIException: Customized IntegrateAI exception for the HTTP Exception.

Client class for interacting with the integrate.ai API.

This class includes functionality for performing various operations using
  the integrate.ai REST API.
This class requires a valid token before it can perform its operations.
